description: Backend data contract for Messages page – direct messaging between school admin and parents/staff.

getThreads.description: Fetch list of conversation threads (ordered by last message time descending).

queryParams.search: string (optional, searches recipient name or student name)

threads.name: string (full name)
threads.student: string (e.g., 'Aarav Sharma (Cls 9-A)', optional for non-parent threads)
threads.avatarColor: string (optional, frontend can compute from name/hash)
threads.unread: number (unread messages count from current admin user's perspective)

threads.name: Mrs. Priya Sharma
threads.lastMessage: Thank you for the update on his attendance.

getMessages.description: Fetch messages for a specific thread. This will also mark all unread messages in that thread as read (for the current admin).

queryParams.before: string (optional, ISO datetime for pagination, fetch older messages)

messages.read: boolean (only relevant for messages from 'them', indicates if admin has read it)

messages.text: Good morning. I wanted to ask about Aarav's attendance this week.
Good morning Mrs. Sharma. Aarav was present all 5 days this week.

sendMessage.description: Send a new message from the admin to the recipient. If the thread does not exist, it may be created implicitly (depends on business logic).

markThreadRead.description: Explicitly mark all unread messages in a thread as read (called when user opens thread, but getMessages already does this implicitly).

getUnreadCount.description: Get total number of unread messages across all threads (for sidebar badge).

searchThreads.description: Search threads by recipient name or student name (identical to getThreads with search param, kept separate for clarity).

fieldNotes.avatarColor: Frontend can generate deterministic colors based on recipientId or name; backend may store but not required.
fieldNotes.online: Optional presence indicator – requires WebSocket or periodic polling; not essential for basic messaging.
fieldNotes.unread: Count of messages sent by 'them' that have not yet been marked as read. getMessages should mark them read automatically.
fieldNotes.read: On message objects from 'them', indicates if admin has read it. For messages from 'me', always true after delivery.
